title: Real Node 22 (NodeSource) replaces bun-node-shim on /usr/local/bin/node
rationale: warren-a82b: the bun-shim symlink (formerly /usr/local/bin/node \u2192 /usr/local/bun-node-fallback-bin/node) double-purposed 'node' for pi compat AND for preview-sidecar JS dev servers. Bun's built-in module coverage drift (e.g. v1.2.23 missing node:sqlite) crashes any Next.js/Remix project at startup. Real Node on PATH closes the door; pi's shebang is patched in-place (sed) to '#!/usr/bin/env bun' so pi still resolves correctly. npm now ships with NodeSource nodejs; we dropped the explicit 'npm@11.14.1' from the bun install -g list. pnpm remains explicit.

name: pi-shebang-patch-at-install
description: When a globally-installed Node CLI (pi-coding-agent) must run under bun even though /usr/local/bin/node is real Node, patch the shebang at image-build time: sed -i '1s|^#!/usr/bin/env node|#!/usr/bin/env bun|' /usr/local/install/global/node_modules/<pkg>/dist/cli.js. Cheaper than moving node off PATH and lets node serve everyone else (npm stubs, pnpm dev servers) as real Node.

content: Bundled os-eco CLIs in the Dockerfile global-install layer (warren-8f64) follow a triple-pin: package.json caret range, bun.lock resolved version, and Dockerfile pinned-major-minor-patch. Adding @os-eco/plot-cli@0.1.1 means all three slots get the same minor; CI release workflow fails on drift between package.json and src/index.ts VERSION but does NOT cross-check the Dockerfile, so bumping plot-cli requires editing both package.json and the RUN bun install -g block, then running bun install to regenerate the lockfile.

Burrow's data dir is pinned onto warren's persistent /data volume via BURROW_DATA_DIR=/data/burrow (Dockerfile ENV + fly.toml [env] mirror, warren-0375). Without it burrow falls back to XDG_DATA_HOME (/root/.local/share/burrow on the container's ephemeral overlay), so every redeploy wipes burrow's SQLite + archive + projects dir and orphans any run in flight: warren's row stays state='running' (its DB is on the volume), but burrow returns 404 for the burrow_run_id on cancel + stream and the bridge retries with backoff indefinitely. BURROW_DATA_DIR is read by burrow-cli's src/config/paths.ts ahead of XDG_DATA_HOME (node_modules/@os-eco/burrow-cli/src/config/paths.ts), and the supervisor's Bun.spawn of 'burrow serve' inherits process env so no supervisor-side wiring change was needed. Burrow's db client mkdir -p's the dbPath parent on first open (db/client.ts), so /data/burrow/ materializes itself on the mounted volume — no Dockerfile mkdir (would be shadowed by the volume mount). 'burrow serve --help' does NOT expose a --data-dir flag at 0.3.2, so env-based pinning is the only knob.
